source_note: rme-roofing.com DNS failed at time of writing — site phrases mirrored from Conversion Exotics universal voice per brief instructions.

slots.2_caller_identity: I review roofing company sites for a living
slots.3_target_buyer: Roofing company owner-operator (B2B — calling roofing company owners to offer CRO services)
slots.4_who_answers: Owner-operator (small shops answer direct), office manager, or crew dispatcher (larger shops)
slots.5_dollar_anchor: $15,000 to $30,000 a week in lost roofing jobs
slots.5_anchor_math: 1-3 missed jobs/wk × $10,000-$18,000 avg residential replacement ticket = $10K-$54K/wk. Conservative anchor $15K-$30K lands credibly without seeming absurd. Sources: ridge-top-exteriors.com (avg $10K-$11K national 2025), green-attic-roofing.com ($6K-$40K range). DFW insurance jobs skew $12K-$18K with hail-belt premium.
slots.5_anchor_upper: $50,000 (1-3 missed hail-belt insurance jobs at $15K-$18K each — use in storm-season calls only)
slots.6_cpc_range: $38 to $75 per click (DFW market — top hail-belt market). Denver CO peaks at $42-$80.
slots.6_cpc_source: market_cpc.json — Google Ads benchmark anchors for residential roofing markets, 2026-05

7_issues_hierarchy.issue: Form/CTA friction (buried, too many steps, competing CTAs)
Phone buried or missing on mobile
7_issues_hierarchy.plain_english: The number they tap on mobile
No hook for the insurance-claim buyer
Nothing that makes you the obvious pick

7_cta_breakdown.STRONG: 178/299 (60%) — prospects with strong CTAs still have trust/form issues
7_cta_breakdown.MODERATE: 96/299 (32%) — moderate CTA = leads leaking at the form step

slots.8_audit_value_rationale: B2B vertical with $10K-$18K per-job ticket size. $3,000 audit value is proportional to anchor and serious enough to command respect from a roofing owner-operator. Mirrors Conversion Exotics at $3,000 (B2C) scaled for B2B stakes.
9_banned_terms: Miner-style apology hedges: 'appreciate you picking up', 'out of the blue', 'I know I'm calling cold'
{company} in beat copy — use 'your site' instead
CTA — use 'how they reach you' / 'the button to call' / 'the phone number they tap'
Hero / hero copy — use 'top of your page' / 'first thing they see'
Above the fold — use 'top of the page'
Conversion rate — use 'how many actually call' / 'how many leads turn into jobs'
Funnel — use 'lead flow' / 'the steps between the ad and the call'
Page speed / TTI / LCP / Core Web Vitals — use 'how fast it loads'
Developer / dev work / dev — frame fixes as 'quick wins' / 'I'd handle that'
Competitor by name — use 'the next contractor' / 'the next shop'
Quick context — BANNED — use 'Here's the deal' / 'Here's what I did' / 'Here's what's going on'
Homeowner language in agent beats — use 'your leads', 'your jobs', 'your close rate', not 'renter taps'
CRO jargon: conversion optimization, A/B test, above-fold, hero section
Agency language: strategy, deliverables, engagement, retainer
10_three_pillars: Top of your page (trust, proof, urgency)
How they reach you (phone tap, form friction)

universal_voice_locks: No {company} in beat copy — use 'your site'
Dual-path close (booked call OR email sample)
B2B register — calling roofing owners, not homeowners

verbatim_site_language.audit_format: Thirty minutes. Your live site on screen.
verbatim_site_language.differentiator: Every leak named in plain English, with dollar-anchored estimates per leak.
verbatim_site_language.dollar_anchored: Dollar-anchored estimates per leak — in jobs per week, not percentages.
verbatim_site_language.yours_to_keep: Yours to keep whether you hire me to ship it or not.
verbatim_site_language.one_week_plan: Sequenced one-week plan — every fix shipped at once, ranked by lead impact.
verbatim_site_language.steal_frame: Flip the steal — so the next homeowner with hail damage calls you, not the next contractor.
verbatim_site_language.cpc_anchor: You're paying $40 to $75 per click — and for a big chunk of those clicks, the lead never turns into a call.

A.name: The Lead Leak
A.hook: Paying $40-$75/click, but lead form is broken or phone is buried — traffic that never becomes a job
A.data_backing: 84% of audited prospects have form/CTA friction; 35% have phone buried on mobile
A.lever: Dollar anchor on wasted ad spend per click
A.best_for: Owners currently running Google Ads — the click spend makes the leak visceral

B.name: Next Contractor Steals It
B.hook: Homeowner has hail damage, taps your ad, slow load or buried phone, taps next contractor, next guy gets the $15K insurance job
B.data_backing: 93% trust/proof issues above fold + 35% phone buried = steal is happening constantly
B.lever: Status loss + FOMO + competitor frame without naming names
B.best_for: Owners in hail-belt markets (DFW, Denver, OKC) where insurance jobs are the high-ticket prize

C.name: 3-Issue Reveal
C.hook: I pulled up your site and found three issues — name two, tease the third
C.data_backing: Top of page (93%) + how they reach you (84%) + third issue held for audit
C.lever: Curiosity gap + loss aversion — hold the worst issue for the audit
C.best_for: Owners with 'we just redesigned' or 'we get leads fine' objection-prone responses

beat_skeleton.1_OPEN: 9-11s — earned-attention hook + dollar anchor + routing question
beat_skeleton.2_PROBLEM: 17-24s — industry context with CPC anchor, ends with question they can't answer well
beat_skeleton.3_AGITATION: 32-40s — concrete walk-through + dollar anchor + tease third issue
beat_skeleton.4_SOLUTION: 15-20s — credibility (100+ sites diagnosed, 10-point checklist, same three leaks)
beat_skeleton.5_AUDIT_OFFER: 33s — $3K audit, free, 30 min shared screen, dollar per leak, one-week plan, yours to keep
beat_skeleton.6_CLOSE: 30s — Thursday 10 or Friday 2, OR sample audit by email

dollar_anchor_math.anchor_used_in_scripts: $15,000 to $30,000 a week in lost roofing jobs
dollar_anchor_math.source_ticket_size: ridgetopexteriors.com 2025 — avg $10K-$11K national; green-attic-roofing.com $6K-$40K range; hail-belt DFW/CO skew $12K-$18K
dollar_anchor_math.source_cpl: searchlightdigital.io Q1 2026 — avg roofing non-branded CPL $124; localiq.com 2025 — roofing & gutters $228 CPL
